ops.title: Server-side eval scoring over local MLX: the E12 Jetsam lesson
ops.body: The watcher retrain lane (`cc_steer/retrain/watcher.py`) scores candidates against the frozen eval via Tinker-side `compute_logprobs` (`score_frame_tinker`, ~$0.13/pass) instead of local MLX inference — deliberately. E12's local scoring pass (641 rows at ~0.45 rows/s on an 8B MLX model, alongside the always-on watcher daemon's model) was killed by macOS Jetsam (`OS_REASON_JETSAM`) at row 90 on a 128GB machine, stranding three successfully-trained cells. The only local inference in the lane is the 20-row serving-parity spot-check, hard-capped by `WatcherRecipe.parity_rows`. Do not "optimize" eval back to local scoring; the constraint is memory pressure, not just wall-clock.
